infobox.value: Black belt in [Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_Jiu-Jitsu "Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu") under Marcus "Conan" Silveira[[2]](#cite_note-tapology.com-2)[[1]](#cite_note-ufc10-1)

(born September 2, 1981) is an Italian professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"), former professional [boxer](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") and [Sanshou](/wiki/Sanshou "Sanshou") kickboxer who competed in the [Light Heavyweight](/wiki/Light_Heavyweight_(MMA) "Light Heavyweight (MMA)") division. He currently is a bare-knuckle boxer for [Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship") where he is the current [BKFC Cruiserweight Champion](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#Cruiserweight_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship"). A professional competitor since 2002, Sakara has also formerly competed for the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), [Bellator MMA](/wiki/Bellator_MMA "Bellator MMA"), [Jungle Fight](/wiki/Jungle_Fight "Jungle Fight"), [Cage Warriors](/wiki/Cage_Warriors "Cage Warriors"), and [M-1 Global](/wiki/M-1_Global "M-1 Global").

**Alessio Sakara** (born September 2, 1981) is an Italian professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"), former professional [boxer](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") and [Sanshou](/wiki/Sanshou "Sanshou") kickboxer who competed in the [Light Heavyweight](/wiki/Light_Heavyweight_(MMA) "Light Heavyweight (MMA)") division. He currently is a bare-knuckle boxer for [Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship") where he is the current [BKFC Cruiserweight Champion](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#Cruiserweight_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship"). A professional competitor since 2002, Sakara has also formerly competed for the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), [Bellator MMA](/wiki/Bellator_MMA "Bellator MMA"), [Jungle Fight](/wiki/Jungle_Fight "Jungle Fight"), [Cage Warriors](/wiki/Cage_Warriors "Cage Warriors"), and [M-1 Global](/wiki/M-1_Global "M-1 Global").

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Born in Rome, Sakara began to compete in soccer at the age of five and was talented, playing the midfielder position.[[3]](#cite_note-mmamania.com-3) He began training in boxing at the age of 11. At the age of 18 Sakara began training with Roberto Almeida[[4]](#cite_note-biography-4)
in [Brazilian jiu-jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu "Brazilian jiu-jitsu"), later earning a black belt under former UFC veteran Marcus "Conan" Silveira. Sakara turned to mixed martial arts after competing in amateur boxing matches because he did not think he would be able to support himself with the little money that boxers in Italy receive, and also did not prefer the tactical approach of the sport.[[5]](#cite_note-pi22-5) Sakara was also attracted to the rising sport of mixed martial arts after his uncle showed him a VHS tape of [UFC 5](/wiki/UFC_5 "UFC 5"), which featured a bout between future [UFC Hall of Famers](/wiki/UFC_Hall_of_Fame "UFC Hall of Fame") [Ken Shamrock](/wiki/Ken_Shamrock "Ken Shamrock") and [Royce Gracie](/wiki/Royce_Gracie "Royce Gracie"), when Sakara was 19 years old.[[6]](#cite_note-training-6) Shortly afterwards in 2000, Sakara relocated to Brazil in order to train with names like Ricardo De La Riva and [Antônio Rogério Nogueira](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Rog%C3%A9rio_Nogueira "Antônio Rogério Nogueira").[[5]](#cite_note-pi22-5)

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

Sakara made his professional mixed martial arts debut in 2002, and compiled a record of 13–3 before being signed by the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship").[[3]](#cite_note-mmamania.com-3)

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Sakara made his UFC debut at [UFC 55](/wiki/UFC_55 "UFC 55"), fighting against Ron Faircloth.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The bout was ruled a no contest due to an inside leg kick to Sakara's groin.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Sakara next fought [Elvis Sinosic](/wiki/Elvis_Sinosic "Elvis Sinosic") at [UFC 57](/wiki/UFC_57 "UFC 57") on February 4, 2006.[[9]](#cite_note-9) He won the bout via a unanimous decision despite having a point deducted due to an illegal elbow.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Sakara was submitted in his next bout at [UFC 60](/wiki/UFC_60 "UFC 60") by decorated [grappling](/wiki/Grappling "Grappling") specialist and former [King of the Cage Middleweight Champion](/wiki/List_of_KOTC_champions "List of KOTC champions") [Dean Lister](/wiki/Dean_Lister "Dean Lister").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Sakara was next expected to face [Wilson Gouveia](/wiki/Wilson_Gouveia "Wilson Gouveia") at [UFC 65](/wiki/UFC_65 "UFC 65") on November 18, 2006.[[12]](#cite_note-12) However, Gouveia withdrew from the bout due to an injury and was replaced by [Drew McFedries](/wiki/Drew_McFedries "Drew McFedries").[[13]](#cite_note-13) Sakara lost the bout via first-round [TKO](/wiki/Technical_knockout "Technical knockout").[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Sakara rebounded from these losses when he fought Victor Valimaki at [UFC 70](/wiki/UFC_70 "UFC 70") on April 21, 2007.[[15]](#cite_note-15) He won via TKO at 1:44 of the first round.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

At [UFC 75](/wiki/UFC_75 "UFC 75") on September 8, 2007, Sakara lost for the third time in four fights, losing via first round TKO to [Houston Alexander](/wiki/Houston_Alexander "Houston Alexander").[[17]](#cite_note-17)

Sakara was part of [UFC 80](/wiki/UFC_80 "UFC 80") on January 19, 2008, where he won via TKO in the first round against veteran James Lee.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

#### Move down to middleweight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Move down to middleweight")]

Following his victory over Lee, Sakara announced that he would be moving down to the [Middleweight](/wiki/Middleweight_(MMA) "Middleweight (MMA)") division.[[19]](#cite_note-sakaraleben-19) He made his middleweight debut against former [WEC Middleweight Champion](/wiki/List_of_WEC_champions#WEC_Middleweight_Championship "List of WEC champions") [Chris Leben](/wiki/Chris_Leben "Chris Leben") at [UFC 82](/wiki/UFC_82 "UFC 82") on March 1, 2008.[[19]](#cite_note-sakaraleben-19) He lost the fight via first-round knockout.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

At [UFC Fight Night 15](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_15 "UFC Fight Night 15"), Sakara defeated [Joe Vedepo](/wiki/Joe_Vedepo "Joe Vedepo") via KO in the first round with a head kick, which earned him *Knockout of the Night* honors.[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-ufn15b-22)

Sakara was then expected to face [Jake Rosholt](/wiki/Jake_Rosholt "Jake Rosholt") at [UFC Fight Night 17](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_17 "UFC Fight Night 17") on February 7, 2009, but the bout was cancelled when Sakara had to pull out due to a shoulder injury and was replaced by [Dan Miller](/wiki/Dan_Miller_(fighter) "Dan Miller (fighter)").[[23]](#cite_note-23)

Sakara was then scheduled to face [Rousimar Palhares](/wiki/Rousimar_Palhares "Rousimar Palhares") at [UFC 99](/wiki/UFC_99 "UFC 99") on June 13, 2009.[[24]](#cite_note-24) However, for unknown reasons the bout was moved to take place at [UFC 101](/wiki/UFC_101 "UFC 101") on August 8, 2009.[[25]](#cite_note-25) Eventually, Palhares ended up withdrawing from the bout due to an injury and was replaced by former title challenger [Thales Leites](/wiki/Thales_Leites "Thales Leites").[[26]](#cite_note-26) Sakara won the bout via split decision.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

The bout with Palhares was re-booked to take place on December 5, 2009, at [The Ultimate Fighter 10 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Heavyweights_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Heavyweights Finale"), but Sakara had to withdraw after suffering an undisclosed injury while training. He was replaced by Lucio Linhares.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

Sakara defeated [James Irvin](/wiki/James_Irvin_(fighter) "James Irvin (fighter)") via first-round TKO on March 21, 2010, at [UFC LIVE: Vera vs. Jones](/wiki/UFC_LIVE:_Vera_vs._Jones "UFC LIVE: Vera vs. Jones").[[29]](#cite_note-29)

Sakara was forced to pull out of his fight with [Nate Marquardt](/wiki/Nate_Marquardt "Nate Marquardt") on July 3, 2010 at [UFC 116](/wiki/UFC_116 "UFC 116") due to the death of his father.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

Sakara was then scheduled to face [Jorge Rivera](/wiki/Jorge_Rivera_(fighter) "Jorge Rivera (fighter)") on August 28, 2010 at [UFC 118](/wiki/UFC_118 "UFC 118"), but Rivera pulled out of the bout with an injury and was replaced by [Gerald Harris](/wiki/Gerald_Harris_(fighter) "Gerald Harris (fighter)")[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32)[[33]](#cite_note-33) In turn, Sakara was forced off the card with an injury and replaced by [Joe Vedepo](/wiki/Joe_Vedepo "Joe Vedepo").[[34]](#cite_note-34)

His bout with Rivera was then rescheduled and was expected to take place on November 13, 2010, at [UFC 122](/wiki/UFC_122 "UFC 122"),[[35]](#cite_note-35) but the fight was cancelled, Sakara intended on competing despite experiencing flu-like symptoms, but the bout was terminated due to Sakara vomiting backstage.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

Sakara was expected to face [Maiquel Falcão](/wiki/Maiquel_Falc%C3%A3o "Maiquel Falcão") on March 3, 2011 at the [UFC Live: Sanchez vs. Kampmann](/wiki/UFC_Live:_Sanchez_vs._Kampmann "UFC Live: Sanchez vs. Kampmann") event.[[37]](#cite_note-37) However, Falcão was injured while training and replaced by [Rafael Natal](/wiki/Rafael_Natal "Rafael Natal").[[38]](#cite_note-38) Then in early February, Natal pulled out of the bout due to a knee injury, and was replaced by promotional newcomer [Chris Weidman](/wiki/Chris_Weidman "Chris Weidman").[[39]](#cite_note-39) Sakara lost a unanimous decision after getting repeatedly taken down and controlled by Weidman's wrestling.[[40]](#cite_note-40)

The bout between Sakara and Rivera was rescheduled again for August 6, 2011 at [UFC 133](/wiki/UFC_133 "UFC 133"), but Sakara was forced out of the bout after tearing the [ACL](/wiki/Anterior_cruciate_ligament "Anterior cruciate ligament") in his knee while training and was replaced by [Costas Philippou](/wiki/Costas_Philippou "Costas Philippou").[[41]](#cite_note-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42)

Sakara faced [Brian Stann](/wiki/Brian_Stann "Brian Stann") on April 14, 2012, at [UFC on Fuel TV: Gustafsson vs. Silva](/wiki/UFC_on_Fuel_TV:_Gustafsson_vs._Silva "UFC on Fuel TV: Gustafsson vs. Silva").[[43]](#cite_note-43) He lost the fight via KO in the first round.[[44]](#cite_note-44)

Sakara lost to [Patrick Côté](/wiki/Patrick_C%C3%B4t%C3%A9_(fighter) "Patrick Côté (fighter)") due to disqualification on November 17, 2012, at [UFC 154](/wiki/UFC_154 "UFC 154") after rocking Côte with elbows, only to get caught up in the moment and land multiple blows to the back of Côté's head.[[45]](#cite_note-45) Sakara's team immediately appealed the result, but the Quebec commission denied the appeal.[[46]](#cite_note-46)

In late November 2012, Sakara signed a new four-fight contract with the UFC.[[47]](#cite_note-47)

A rematch was briefly linked with Côté for March 16, 2013 at [UFC 158](/wiki/UFC_158 "UFC 158").[[48]](#cite_note-48) However, Sakara was forced out of the bout with a kidney illness.[[49]](#cite_note-49)

Sakara was slated to face [Tom Watson](/wiki/Tom_Watson_(fighter) "Tom Watson (fighter)") at [UFC Fight Night 30](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_30 "UFC Fight Night 30").[[50]](#cite_note-50) However, Watson was forced out of the bout with an injury and was replaced by [Magnus Cedenblad](/wiki/Magnus_Cedenblad "Magnus Cedenblad").[[51]](#cite_note-51) Subsequently in early October, Cedenblad was forced out of the Sakara bout with an injury and replaced by newcomer [Nico Musoke](/wiki/Nico_Musoke "Nico Musoke").[[52]](#cite_note-52) Sakara lost the fight by submission due to an armbar in the first round and was released from the promotion shortly after.[[53]](#cite_note-53)

### Final Fight Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Final Fight Championship")]

After a nine-month delay, on July 24, 2014, Sakara signed a three-fight deal with the European promotion [Final Fight Championship](/wiki/Final_Fight_Championship "Final Fight Championship").[[54]](#cite_note-Alessio_Sakara_signes_for_FFC!-54) During the press conference he stated that his UFC career was compromised because he had enormous trouble cutting weight at 84 kg (185 pounds) and that he will continue his career in the Light Heavyweight division.

Sakara's Final Fight Championship debut took place on December 6, 2014 against Maciej Browarski.[[55]](#cite_note-55) The fight was stopped in the first round when Sakara wasn't able to continue after getting injured from tearing his bicep. First Browarski was awarded the victory via TKO (injury), the result was later overturned to a No Contest by the Croatian MMA Federation.[[56]](#cite_note-56)

In his next fight, Sakara faced off against [Lebanon](/wiki/Lebanon "Lebanon")'s Dib Akil. Following the opening seconds of the round, in which Akil threw wild hooks that unsuccessfully landed, Sakara took Akil to the ground and piled up punches until the TKO stoppage.

### Bellator MMA

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Bellator MMA")]

On November 27, 2015, it was announced that Sakara signed a three-fight contract with [Bellator MMA](/wiki/Bellator_MMA "Bellator MMA").[[57]](#cite_note-57)

Sakara made his promotional debut against [Brian Rogers](/wiki/Brian_Rogers_(fighter) "Brian Rogers (fighter)") at Bellators first event in Sakara's native [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy") on April 16, 2016 at [Bellator 152](/wiki/Bellator_MMA_in_2016 "Bellator MMA in 2016").[[58]](#cite_note-58) He won via knockout in the second round.[[59]](#cite_note-59)

On September 21, 2016, it was announced that Sakara would be facing [Joey Beltran](/wiki/Joey_Beltran "Joey Beltran") in the co-main event of [Bellator 168](/wiki/Bellator_MMA_in_2016#Bellator_168 "Bellator MMA in 2016") on December 10, 2016.[[60]](#cite_note-60) He won via knockout in the first round.[[61]](#cite_note-61)

#### Bellator title shot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Bellator title shot")]

Sakara challenged [Rafael Carvalho](/wiki/Rafael_Carvalho "Rafael Carvalho") for the [Bellator Middleweight Championship](/wiki/List_of_Bellator_MMA_champions#Middleweight_World_Championship "List of Bellator MMA champions") on December 9, 2017 at [Bellator 190](/wiki/Bellator_MMA_in_2017#Bellator_190 "Bellator MMA in 2017").[[62]](#cite_note-62) He lost via knockout in the first round.[[63]](#cite_note-63)

#### Post title shot reign

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Post title shot reign")]

Sakara headlined [Bellator 211](/wiki/Bellator_MMA_in_2018 "Bellator MMA in 2018") against Kent Kauppinen on December 1, 2018.[[64]](#cite_note-64) He lost the fight via knockout in the first round.[[65]](#cite_note-65)

Sakara faced Canaan Grigsby at [Bellator Milan](/wiki/Bellator_MMA_in_2019#Bellator_Milan "Bellator MMA in 2019") on October 12, 2019.[[66]](#cite_note-66) He won the fight via TKO in the first round.[[67]](#cite_note-67)

Sakara was scheduled to face Darwin Rodriguez at [Bellator Milan 3](/wiki/2020_in_Bellator_MMA#Bellator_Milan_3 "2020 in Bellator MMA") on October 3, 2020.[[68]](#cite_note-68) However, Sakara ended up withdrawing from the bout due to an undisclosed injury.[[69]](#cite_note-69)

On July 10, 2021, it was announced that he was no longer under contract with Bellator.[[70]](#cite_note-70) However, in early 2023 news surfaced that Sakara was still under contract with Bellator.[[71]](#cite_note-mmaj23-71)

## Bare-knuckle boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Bare-knuckle boxing")]

In February 2023, it was announced that Sakara had signed a multi-fight contract with [Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship").[[71]](#cite_note-mmaj23-71)

Sakara faced Erick Lozano on July 25, 2025 at [BKFC Fight Night 27](/wiki/BKFC_Fight_Night_27 "BKFC Fight Night 27").[[72]](#cite_note-72) He won the fight by knockout in the second round.[[73]](#cite_note-73)

Sakara competed for the [BKFC Cruiserweight Championship](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#Cruiserweight_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship") against current champion [Chris Camozzi](/wiki/Chris_Camozzi "Chris Camozzi") on October 25, 2025 at [BKFC 83](/wiki/2025_in_Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#BKFC_83_Italy:_Camozzi_vs._Sakara "2025 in Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship").[[74]](#cite_note-74) He won the championship by split decision.[[75]](#cite_note-75)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Sakara has two sons from a previous marriage. Recognizable for his many tattoos, Sakara has the words "Senatus Populusque Romanus" on his forearm, a common marking of soldiers of the [Roman Empire](/wiki/Roman_Empire "Roman Empire"), as well as several other tattoos that are a tribute to his ethnic background and love of Roman history.[[3]](#cite_note-mmamania.com-3)[[76]](#cite_note-76) His surname comes from [Saqqara](/wiki/Saqqara "Saqqara"), which was a [Roman colony](/wiki/Roman_colony "Roman colony") in Egypt.[[77]](#cite_note-77)

### Autobiography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alessio_Sakara&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Autobiography")]

In May 2020, a book in Italian about Sakara – *Ogni giorno in battaglia. La mentalità del legionario* – was published ([ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-8885493957](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-8885493957 "Special:BookSources/978-8885493957")).
